_comment: VERY HARD SkillOpt benchmark — tasks with real algorithmic content (operator precedence, multi-filter state, sorting, scoring, pagination) where a 4B model is most likely to slip. gemma4 aced the first 'harder' set (tip/chained-calc/wizard/filter/stopwatch) at 1.000, so this set escalates. Checks lean on computed values + selector-independent 'changed'. Use via OXY_SO_TASKS=skillopt/tasks-hard.json.

train.prompt: Build a currency converter in a single index.html with vanilla JS: a dollars number input and a 'Convert' button; on click it shows the euro amount (dollars times 0.9). Then call done.
Build a countdown timer: a number input for seconds and a 'Start' button that counts down to 0, updating every second. Vanilla JS, single index.html. Then call done.

expect.label: the countdown ticks down after Start

val.prompt: Build a calculator in a single index.html with vanilla JS: digit buttons and operator buttons +, −, ×, ÷ that respect operator PRECEDENCE (multiply and divide bind tighter than add and subtract), plus = and Clear. So 2 + 3 × 4 = must give 14, not 20. Then call done.

Build a to-do app in a single index.html with vanilla JS: a text input + Add button; each item has a checkbox to complete it and a delete button; and filter buttons 'All', 'Active', 'Completed' that show only the matching items. Then call done.

expect.label: the Completed filter hides the active items

val.prompt: Build a sortable table in a single index.html with vanilla JS: a table of three people — Charlie 30, Alice 50, Bob 10 (columns: name, score) — and a 'Sort by score' button that reorders the rows ascending by score. Then call done.
Build a 3-question multiple-choice quiz in a single index.html with vanilla JS. Each question has selectable options, and a Submit button at the end shows the score formatted as N/3. Then call done.

expect.label: Submit shows a score out of 3

val.prompt: Build a paginated list in a single index.html with vanilla JS: 12 numbered items shown 5 per page, with Prev and Next buttons and a 'Page X of 3' indicator that updates. Then call done.

expect.label: Next advances to the next page of items
